patches.find: the hardcoded expected value sits in the same commit as the live fetch call, making even that weaker test unverifiable
patches.replace: no monitor.py entry exists for this tidal constituent at all — the dome author has not included O1 in the verification suite — making even that weaker timestamp test inapplicable: there is nothing to timestamp before the data arrives
patches.rationale: WIN-050 has code_analysis.monitoring='none' (no monitoring code in monitor.py for O1 tidal period), but prose claimed 'live fetch call'. Confirmed in current monitor.py: O1 has no domain entry. The stronger framing (nothing to timestamp) is more accurate and more damaging to the dome's claim.

patches.find: the hardcoded expected value sits in the same commit as the live fetch call, making even that weaker test unverifiable
patches.replace: no monitor.py entry exists for this tidal constituent at all — the dome author has not included N2 in the verification suite — making even that weaker timestamp test inapplicable: there is nothing to timestamp before the data arrives

patches.find: the hardcoded expected value sits in the same commit as the live fetch call, making even that weaker test unverifiable
patches.replace: both the predicted value (LUNAR_H/2 = 24.84/2) and observed value (12.4206 h) are hardcoded static constants in monitor.py with no live data fetch at runtime, making even that weaker test unverifiable: the "observed" value was fixed at deployment, not pulled from a live source after the fact
patches.rationale: WIN-045 monitoring='hardcoded' confirmed in monitor.py (domain 4: m2_pred = LUNAR_H/2, m2_obs = 12.4206 — both constants). The 'live fetch call' language was inaccurate; no fetch call exists in this domain. Cycle-2 'none' tag was stale — dome added tidal monitoring after 2026-04-07.
